title: Common Table Expressions (CTE)
content:
### Common Table Expressions (CTE)

A CTE is a temporary, named result set defined within a query using the WITH clause. It improves query organization and readability.

---

### CTE Syntax

```sql
WITH cte_name AS (
    SELECT ...
    FROM ...
    WHERE ...
)
SELECT * FROM cte_name;
```

### Basic CTE Example

```sql
WITH high_earners AS (
    SELECT name, salary, department
    FROM employees
    WHERE salary > 80000
)
SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS count
FROM high_earners
GROUP BY department;
```

### Multiple CTEs

```sql
WITH
dept_stats AS (
    SELECT department,
           AVG(salary) AS avg_salary,
           COUNT(*) AS emp_count
    FROM employees
    GROUP BY department
),
high_depts AS (
    SELECT department
    FROM dept_stats
    WHERE avg_salary > 70000
)
SELECT e.name, e.salary, e.department
FROM employees e
WHERE e.department IN (SELECT department FROM high_depts);
```

### CTE vs Subquery

| Aspect | CTE | Subquery |
|--------|-----|----------|
| Readability | Named, can be referenced multiple times | Inline, harder to read when complex |
| Reusability | Can be referenced multiple times in the same query | Must be repeated |
| Recursion | Supported (recursive CTE) | Not supported |
| Scope | Only within the current query | Only within the current clause |

### CTE in INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE

```sql
WITH updated_employees AS (
    UPDATE employees
    SET salary = salary * 1.1
    WHERE department = 'Engineering'
    RETURNING *
)
SELECT * FROM updated_employees;
```

> **Key Point:** CTEs make complex queries more readable by breaking them into named, logical steps. They are especially useful when the same subquery is referenced multiple times.
codeExample:
WITH dept_average AS (
    SELECT department, AVG(salary) AS avg_sal
    FROM employees
    GROUP BY department
)
SELECT e.name, e.salary, d.avg_sal
FROM employees e
JOIN dept_average d ON e.department = d.department
WHERE e.salary > d.avg_sal;

quiz.question: What is an advantage of CTE over subqueries?
options: CTEs can be referenced multiple times in the same query
